commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=ea5986645655c58b6ddb1d9edf61296a3f484d16)
commands.review_summary:
The PR propagates outbound payload target metadata into delivery outcomes and makes subagent/ACP completion delivery accounting require requester-route target evidence.

PR surface: Source +269, Tests +635. Total +904 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main accepts broad visible-payload or messaging-tool evidence without requester-route matching, while current docs require completion delivery to keep the resolved requester route. I did not run tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blockers are contributor/maintainer real behavior proof and upgrade-safety judgment for stricter threaded delivery accounting, which an automated repair lane cannot supply.

target.title: fix(agents): verify completion delivery target
target.body:
## Summary

This PR tightens ACP/subagent completion announcement delivery accounting so a completion is only counted as visibly delivered when the delivery evidence matches the requester route.

It:
- propagates outbound payload target metadata into delivery status
- validates automatic completion delivery against provider/account/channel/thread targets
- requires exact account/thread evidence for threaded origins
- preserves target metadata on failed payload outcomes for diagnostics
- keeps the current-chat generated-media compatibility path while avoiding explicit wrong-target matches

## Behavior addressed

Completion announcements can be injected through an internal/inter-session path while the original requester is a channel/thread. Broad checks such as “visible payload exists” or “message tool sent something” can count delivery as successful even when the visible delivery evidence belongs to another route.

This change makes completion delivery proof target-aware: wrong thread, missing thread, missing account, and provider mismatches no longer satisfy delivery for a concrete requester route.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** After this patch, ACP/subagent completion delivery is counted as visibly delivered only when the delivery evidence matches the requester route. Wrong provider/account/channel/thread evidence no longer satisfies delivery for a concrete requester route.
- **Real environment tested:** Non-production isolated source checkout on current upstream `main`. No production/live Gateway runtime changes were made.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. Ran the focused completion-delivery regression tests with fast test mode enabled:

     ```bash
     OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 vitest run src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts src/agents/command/delivery.test.ts src/channels/message/send.test.ts --maxWorkers=1
     ```

  2. Ran core typecheck: `pnpm tsgo:core`.
  3. Ran formatting check on the touched files with `oxfmt --check --threads=1`.
  4. Ran `git diff --check`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Focused regression tests passed:

  ```text
  5 files passed
  261 tests passed
  ```

  Additional checks passed:

  ```text
  tsgo:core: passed
  oxfmt --check touched files: passed
  git diff --check: passed
  ```

- **Observed result:** The regression tests cover message-tool completion evidence delivered to the wrong thread, evidence missing the requester thread/account, automatic completion delivery with wrong thread, and automatic completion delivery with target metadata preserved on failure. Those cases no longer count as visible completion for the original requester route.
- **What was not tested:** No live production Gateway or live Discord proof was run. The proof is based on deterministic non-production regression tests plus type/format/diff checks; maintainers can require live threaded-channel proof or apply an explicit proof override if source-level evidence is sufficient.

## Exact steps or command run after this patch

```bash
<repo>/../workspace/.github/scripts/openclaw-heavy-run --backend auto -- \
  env OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 \
  /bin/sh pnpm exec vitest run \
  src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts \
  src/agents/command/delivery.test.ts \
  src/channels/message/send.test.ts \
  --maxWorkers=1

<repo>/../workspace/.github/scripts/openclaw-heavy-run --backend auto -- \
  /bin/sh pnpm tsgo:core

/bin/sh pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 \
  src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts \
  src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts \
  src/agents/command/delivery.ts \
  src/agents/command/delivery.test.ts \
  src/channels/message/send.ts \
  src/channels/message/send.test.ts \
  src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts \
  src/infra/outbound/deliver-types.ts

git diff --check
```

## Evidence after fix

- Focused tests: 5 files passed, 261 tests passed
- `tsgo:core`: passed
- `oxfmt --check`: passed
- `git diff --check`: passed

## Observed result after fix

The regression tests now cover:
- message-tool completion evidence delivered to the wrong thread
- message-tool completion evidence missing the requester thread/account
- automatic completion delivery with wrong thread
- automatic completion delivery with targetless evidence for a threaded requester
- automatic completion delivery missing requester thread/account
- automatic completion delivery to a different provider
- exact-thread positive delivery
- current-chat generated-media telemetry compatibility
- target serialization on sent/failed payload outcomes

## What was not tested

This PR does not use a live production gateway or a real Discord workspace. The validation is isolated source-level regression coverage plus core typecheck.
